TimeEntry.Title: Weekly Firewall Backup – B2I
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary

Performed the weekly firewall backup for B2i. Logged in to the firewall, downloaded the latest configuration backup file, and uploaded the backup to the Teams channel for recordkeeping and reference. Verified that the backup completed successfully.

TimeEntry.Title: Weekly Firewall Backup – B2I
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary

Performed the weekly firewall backup for B2i. Logged in to the firewall, downloaded the latest configuration backup file, and uploaded the backup to the Teams channel for recordkeeping and reference. Verified that the backup completed successfully.

TimeEntry.Title: Laptop-JonM,LPT-EREAD Missing patch while last run
TimeEntry.Notes:
I worked on the Missing patches and logged into the ManageEngine portal to review the approved patches for the machines. I downloaded and manually installed the applicable failed patches, then rebooted . Also scanned the machines. Some patches not applicable due to non compatibility. All previously missing patches were successfully updated.Machines are  Laptop-JonM,LPT-EREAD

Patches are Raptor Lake-P [Iris Xe Graphics][PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_A7A0] (32.0.101.7077)
TreeSize Free (4.8.1.609)

TimeEntry.Title: IDEA-PC : Manual failed patch update as APD failed to do
TimeEntry.Notes: Accessed the client machine, downloaded the patch using the reference provided by MyRMM, and applied it successfully. Rebooted the machine as required to complete the implementation. After the reboot, I scanned the system again through MyRMM, and the patches were confirmed as successfully applied. Other patches were initiated but could not be completed, as I no longer had access to the machine after the reboot.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: IDEA-PC : Manual failed patch update as APD failed to do
TimeEntry.Notes: Accessed the client machine, downloaded the patch using the reference provided by MyRMM, and applied it successfully. Rebooted the machine as required to complete the implementation. After the reboot, I scanned the system again through MyRMM, and the patches were confirmed as successfully applied. Other patches were initiated but could not be completed, as I no longer had access to the machine after the reboot.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Notes: After checking the CrowdStrike portal, I found that  Crowdstrike latest version updated B2I-3CX-LOANER & LAPTOP-CO9S1K5I  needed updates. I updated both to version 7.32.20403.0

TimeEntry.Title: Checked and confirmed the ManageEngine agent version on the B2I system.
TimeEntry.Notes: Checked and confirmed the ManageEngine agent version on the B2I system.

TimeEntry.Title: Identified Synchronization issues in ManageEngine LAPTOP-CO9S1K5I,B2I-3CX-LOANER
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the ManageEngine portal and verified sync status for devices LAPTOP-CO9S1K5I,B2I-3CX-LOANER Checked communication between endpoint agents and the ManageEngine server.Performed troubleshooting steps to restore connectivity.Re-initiated synchronization and confirmed successful communication.Current Status:Synchronization has been successfully restored. All devices are now reporting and syncing properly in ManageEngine.

TimeEntry.Title: Identified Synchronization issues in ManageEngine LAPTOP-CO9S1K5I,B2I-3CX-LOANER
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the ManageEngine portal and verified sync status for devices LAPTOP-CO9S1K5I,B2I-3CX-LOANER Checked communication between endpoint agents and the ManageEngine server.Performed troubleshooting steps to restore connectivity.Re-initiated synchronization and confirmed successful communication.Current Status:Synchronization has been successfully restored. All devices are now reporting and syncing properly in ManageEngine.

TimeEntry.Title: Firewall : Weekly backup (JAN-2026)
TimeEntry.Notes: The backup has been taken and uploaded to the B2I Teams channel under the Backup folder.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Update failed patches on the PONO & Victoria Computers
TimeEntry.Notes:
As part of scheduled patching and security maintenance, accessed the systems to review pending updates. Successfully installed the required application updates to ensure security, performance, and compliance.

Updates applied:

  • Microsoft 365 Apps for Business – Current Channel (x64), Version 2512 (Build 19530.20138)

  • Google Chrome (x64) updated to 143.0.7499.192 / 143.0.7499.193

  • Zoom Workplace (x64, User-based) updated to Version 6.7.2.26346

Post-installation verification was completed, and both systems are now compliant with the latest update requirements.

TimeEntry.Title: New system setup for Cole Randall
TimeEntry.Notes: Received a support call from Cole Randall to set up a new laptop. Assisted him with the initial laptop setup and guided him to create a remote session. Once connected, installed the Technijian tools on the laptop. After completing the installation, removed his personal account, created a local account, and configured his official email. Informed the user once the setup was completed

TimeEntry.Title: LPT-DEREK & B2I-3CX-LOANER : Manually update failed patches (compatible)
TimeEntry.Notes: I downloaded all the packages provided by MyRMM and attempted to apply them. Only patches 112768 and 43085 were compatible, and both were applied successfully and now show as updated. After the update, I also scanned the MyRMM database, where these patches were reflected as updated.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: LPT-DEREK & B2I-3CX-LOANER : Manually update failed patches (compatible)
TimeEntry.Notes: I downloaded all the packages provided by MyRMM and attempted to apply them. Only patches 112768 and 43085 were compatible, and both were applied successfully and now show as updated. After the update, I also scanned the MyRMM database, where these patches were reflected as updated.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Emails Not Sending from HawkSoft – COLEB2
TimeEntry.Notes: User reported that emails were not sending from HawkSoft. Connected to the COLEB2 system and found that Outlook Classic was not installed. Installed Outlook Classic and tested, but the same error persisted. Performed troubleshooting; however, the issue was not resolved. Requested the user to check with the HawkSoft support team. User confirmed they will coordinate with HawkSoft and will inform us if further assistance is needed.

TimeEntry.Title: Update missing patch on PONO Computer
TimeEntry.Notes:
As per the patch management review, the following updates were identified and applied on the affected system (PONO):

  • Microsoft 365 Apps for Business – Current Channel (x64)
    Version: 2512 (Build 19530.20144)

  • Google Chrome (x64)
    Versions: 144.0.7559.59, 144.0.7559.60

A post-update scan was performed from the ManageEngine console to clear the patch status.

TimeEntry.Title: Update failed patches on the Victoria
TimeEntry.Notes:
During the patch management review, the following updates failed to install on the system (Victoria):

  • Google Chrome (x64)
    Versions: 144.0.7559.59, 144.0.7559.60

  • Microsoft 365 Apps for Enterprise – Current Channel (x64)
    Version: 2512 (Build 19530.20144)

The failure has been noted and will be reattempted after further troubleshooting and verification.

TimeEntry.Title: Microsoft Error Again
TimeEntry.Notes: during the remote session, the reported issue has been resolved successfully. Verified functionality with the user. Hence closing this ticket

TimeEntry.Title: Reviewed CrowdStrike and verified the CrowdStrike agent version on the B2I system.
TimeEntry.Notes: Reviewed CrowdStrike and verified the CrowdStrike agent version on the B2I system.

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version(7.33.20505.0)updated on machines B2I-3CX-LOANER,B2I-HQ-WINEDGE,LAPTOP-CO9S1K5I,LAPTOP-EGS0JV44
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login into Crowd strike portal to check the machines with outdated version. Found machines B2I-3CX-LOANER,B2I-HQ-WINEDGE,LAPTOP-CO9S1K5I,LAPTOP-EGS0JV44
running with older version of crowd strike. So connected to machines and uninstalled the old version  and downloaded and installed the latest sensor version 7.33.20505.0
of CrowdStrike, verified from the portal. it is updated now. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version(7.33.20505.0)updated on machines B2I-3CX-LOANER,B2I-HQ-WINEDGE,LAPTOP-CO9S1K5I,LAPTOP-EGS0JV44
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login into Crowd strike portal to check the machines with outdated version. Found machines B2I-3CX-LOANER,B2I-HQ-WINEDGE,LAPTOP-CO9S1K5I,LAPTOP-EGS0JV44
running with older version of crowd strike. So connected to machines and uninstalled the old version  and downloaded and installed the latest sensor version 7.33.20505.0
of CrowdStrike, verified from the portal. it is updated now. Hence closing this ticket.

Worked on the Missing patches and logged into the ManageEngine portal to review the approved patches for the machines. I downloaded and manually installed the applicable failed/missing patches. machines rebooted.Some patches not deployed due to not compability.Scanned the machines All previously missing patches were successfully updated.

• Logged into ManageEngine and found that the device Victoria was pending for reboot.

  • Logged into the computer and performed a scan for patches.

  • The computer was rebooted to complete pending updates.

TimeEntry.Title: Update Missing patch on the computer Jared-Friedman-PC
TimeEntry.Notes:
As per patch management review, a pending driver update was identified on the system. The patch could not be deployed successfully through the portal and remains outstanding.

Pending / Failed patch:

  • Meteor Lake PCH CNVi WiFi
    PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_7E40 – Version 23.170.0.1

The update may require manual installation Post-installation scanning will be required to clear the patch from the portal.

TimeEntry.Title: Cisco Umbrella synchronization Issue with the system.
TimeEntry.Notes:
Logged into the Cisco Umbrella portal to verify that all systems for B2I are properly synchronized and listed as expected.

Verified the synchronization status of the systems and deleted the old system and rescanned the services. 

Also, checked that the latest version of the Cisco Umbrella Secure Client is installed on all systems.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: Set up outlook
TimeEntry.Notes: as per the email Connected to the user’s home computer remotely. Updated Microsoft Office 365 and configured Outlook successfully. Outlook is currently syncing due to the large OST file size. Informed the user via email that the syncing process may take some time to complete.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: Set up outlook
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary:

Issue:
Move the Outlook profile to the D: drive as the C: drive has low capacity. The Outlook profile got corrupted during the move, and the user required a new profile to be set up.

Action Taken:

  1. Method 1: Created a new Outlook profile:

    • Closed Outlook and opened Control Panel > Mail > Show Profiles.

    • Created a new profile and entered email account details.

    • Configured Outlook Data File Settings by selecting the old OST file (or new location) and disabling offline use.

    • Enabled Cached Exchange Mode and restarted Outlook.

    • Outcome: This method did not work as expected, and the profile failed to load correctly.

  2. Troubleshooting:

    • Attempted to repair the application both offline and online, followed by a reboot, but the issue persisted.

    • Cleaned up the registry and tried again, but the issue still occurred.

  3. Method 2: Edited the Windows Registry:

    • Closed Outlook and opened the Registry Editor (regedit).

    • Navigated to HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Office\xx.0\Outlook (xx.0 being the Office version).

    • Created a new Expandable String Value named ForceOSTPath and set it to the desired location (e.g., D:\OutlookFiles\).

    • Restarted the computer and opened Outlook, but the issue persisted.

  4. Method 3: Used Symbolic Links (Successful):

    • To resolve the issue, the MKLINK command was used to create a symbolic link between the original and new paths:

      • Executed the command:
        mklink %localappdata%\Microsoft\Outlook\<file name> "D:\Outlook Data Files\<file name>"

    • This method successfully redirected the OST file to the D: drive and resolved the issue.

Outcome:
The Outlook profile was successfully moved to the D: drive using Symbolic Links. The issue is now resolved, and the profile is functioning correctly.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: Set up outlook
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary:

Issue:
Move the Outlook profile to the D: drive as the C: drive has low capacity. The Outlook profile got corrupted during the move, and the user required a new profile to be set up.

Action Taken:

  1. Method 1: Created a new Outlook profile:

    • Closed Outlook and opened Control Panel > Mail > Show Profiles.

    • Created a new profile and entered email account details.

    • Configured Outlook Data File Settings by selecting the old OST file (or new location) and disabling offline use.

    • Enabled Cached Exchange Mode and restarted Outlook.

    • Outcome: This method did not work as expected, and the profile failed to load correctly.

  2. Troubleshooting:

    • Attempted to repair the application both offline and online, followed by a reboot, but the issue persisted.

    • Cleaned up the registry and tried again, but the issue still occurred.

  3. Method 2: Edited the Windows Registry:

    • Closed Outlook and opened the Registry Editor (regedit).

    • Navigated to HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Office\xx.0\Outlook (xx.0 being the Office version).

    • Created a new Expandable String Value named ForceOSTPath and set it to the desired location (e.g., D:\OutlookFiles\).

    • Restarted the computer and opened Outlook, but the issue persisted.

  4. Method 3: Used Symbolic Links (Successful):

    • To resolve the issue, the MKLINK command was used to create a symbolic link between the original and new paths:

      • Executed the command:
        mklink %localappdata%\Microsoft\Outlook\<file name> "D:\Outlook Data Files\<file name>"

    • This method successfully redirected the OST file to the D: drive and resolved the issue.

Outcome:
The Outlook profile was successfully moved to the D: drive using Symbolic Links. The issue is now resolved, and the profile is functioning correctly.

TimeEntry.Title: RHEA-DELL2: Re: Set up outlook
TimeEntry.Notes: I found that although Outlook was functioning properly, sent emails were not appearing in Outlook while they were visible in Gmail. Initial troubleshooting did not resolve the issue. Further investigation showed that the relevant option in Outlook was disabled. After enabling it, the user is now able to see sent emails in Outlook.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: RHEA-DELL2: Re: Set up outlook
TimeEntry.Notes: I found that although Outlook was functioning properly, sent emails were not appearing in Outlook while they were visible in Gmail. Initial troubleshooting did not resolve the issue. Further investigation showed that the relevant option in Outlook was disabled. After enabling it, the user is now able to see sent emails in Outlook.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote
